title: Air-fryer roasted broccoli
subtitle: Charred, crisp-edged florets in 12 minutes
excerpt: Broccoli florets tossed in olive oil and garlic powder, cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 10 to 12 minutes. The high circulating heat chars the floret edges and crisps the smaller pieces in a way that boiling or steaming cannot produce.
sourceNotes: Roasted broccoli became widely popular in Britain in the 2010s as a reaction against overcooked, waterlogged boiled broccoli. High-heat dry cooking transforms broccoli florets: the smaller pieces char at their tips and the stems become tender, producing a result that is entirely different in character from any wet-heat cooking method.

content.text: Dry the broccoli thoroughly before oiling. Broccoli florets trap water in their rough texture. Any moisture left on the surface will steam the florets rather than roast them, producing a soft green result instead of charred edges. Spin in a salad spinner after washing, then spread on a clean tea towel for 5 minutes.

items.prepNote: cut into medium florets, stems included and halved lengthways

content.text: Dry the {{broccoli}} florets thoroughly. Toss with {{olive-oil}}, {{garlic-powder}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, {{black-pepper}}, and {{chilli-flakes}} if using until evenly coated. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 2 minutes. Spread in the basket in a single layer. Cook at 200°C for 10 to 12 minutes, shaking the basket once at the 6-minute mark, until the edges of the florets are lightly charred and the stems are tender when pierced. Squeeze over {{lemon}} before serving.

items.symptom: Broccoli soft and steamed rather than charred
items.cause: Not dried before oiling, or basket overcrowded
items.fix: Any surface moisture prevents charring. Dry thoroughly after washing. Spread in a single layer with space between florets; a crowded basket creates steam, not char

items.symptom: Some florets burnt, others still raw
items.cause: Florets cut to very different sizes
items.fix: Aim for consistent floret sizes. Small pieces from the edges of the head are fine but should be placed on top rather than directly on the basket. Larger florets take longer; cut them in half through the stalk

items.symptom: Broccoli very dry and stringy after cooking
items.cause: Cooked too long, or very small florets
items.fix: Check at 10 minutes. Broccoli goes from perfectly charred to dry quickly. The stems are the best indicator: tender when pressed with a knife tip means it is ready

content.text: Air-fryer roasted broccoli is a fast side dish that converts people who dislike boiled broccoli. Serve alongside salmon, steak, or chicken, or tossed through pasta with parmesan and lemon. A tablespoon of tahini or a handful of toasted almonds added at the table turns it into a salad.
